title: Tab child rendering — all children render stacked instead of showing only the active tab
description: All framework wrappers (Angular, React, Vue, Svelte) fall through to a generic children loop after rendering the tabs component. The result is all child configs rendered stacked below the tab bar, regardless of which tab is active. Root cause: SafeRendererComponent (and equivalents) checks childMode !== 'component' and renders all children unconditionally. SafeTabsComponent does not own child rendering — it delegates to the builder which outputs an empty data-tab-content div with no child mounted. Fix required in all four framework wrappers: SafeTabs must intercept child rendering, track active tab state, and mount only the active child config. The generic children loop must not fire for tabs. The builder-render proof (573 checks) passes because it only validates DOM structure, not which children are visible. A new prove assertion is needed: for tabs, only the active child's data-tab-key matches the active tab key, and no other children are in the DOM.
acceptance: Clicking a tab shows only that tab's child content
All other children are not in the DOM (not hidden, not rendered)
defaultActive tab is shown on first render
Tab bar remains visible during child swap
Fix applied to: Angular 21, Angular 22, React 18, React 19, Vue 2, Vue 3, Svelte 4, Svelte 5
resolution: safecontracts: tabs added to CHILD_MODES as 'component'. Framework fixes: React 18/19 (SafeTabsReact in SafeRenderer), Angular 21/22 (own DOM, stable listeners), Vue 3 (buildComponent per active child, watch active), Svelte 4/5 ($effect/$afterUpdate + buildComponent). Root cause: ctx.fire routes async through EPRPP — Angular/Vue2 must own DOM directly. Vue 2 verification blocked by pre-existing viewer TS compile error (viewer-0001). 7/8 frameworks confirmed working.
